div.h1: Lock-free vs Mutex Throughput Under Contention

p.strong: Benchmark: Hash Map Operations (50% insert, 30% lookup, 20% delete)

div.p: Below 4 threads: Mutex overhead is acceptable

ul.li: Rare blocking, good cache behavior Above 8 threads: Lock-free becomes essential
Lock convoys form under burst traffic
Lock-free CAS backoff prevents thundering herd

div.p: Mutex wins ~20% Lower overhead without contention Simpler code path, better cache locality
Lock-Free wins ~50% Contention begins, lock wait times increase CAS failures still rare
Lock-Free wins ~100% Mutex contention spikes, threads queue Lock-free scales via parallel CAS attempts
Lock-Free wins ~180% Mutex: severe lock convoying Lock-free: CAS backoff prevents starvation
Lock-Free wins ~300% Mutex: threads spend most time waiting Lock-free: progress guaranteed per operation
Throughput scales with threads until contention dominates
